steps: Launch via pwsh scripts/launch-toaster-monitored.ps1 -ObservationSeconds 120.
Open Settings -> Captions.
Confirm the preview pane shows a frame outline with rounded corners, horizontal + vertical centerlines, and double-headed arrows on each outer edge.
Confirm there is NO photographic background image of any kind.
In the running app DevTools inspect the SVG strokes used by CaptionMockFrame.
Confirm stroke colors map to the existing tokens (#EEEEEE rest, #E8A838 accent on hover or focus); no invented greys or reds.
Inspect the rendered preview frame visually and via DOM.
Confirm there are no <text> SVG nodes with pixel-dimension strings (no '1920x1080', '480x800', etc.).
Confirm only the caption pill text is rendered as text inside the frame area.
Open Settings -> Captions.
Locate the Orientation select; confirm it has options Horizontal and Vertical.
Toggle to Vertical; confirm the preview pane re-renders to a 9:16 aspect (taller than wide) without reloading the page.
Toggle back to Horizontal; confirm the pane returns to 16:9.
With caption_position = 90, caption_font_size = 40 (defaults), confirm the caption pill in Horizontal sits near the bottom of the frame.
Toggle to Vertical; confirm the pill is still positioned at the same proportional location (~10% from the bottom of the new vertical frame).
Confirm pill visual font size on screen is comparable (within ~20%) across the two orientations - no orientation-induced 1.78x scale jump.
Set caption_max_width_percent = 100 and caption_font_size = 72; switch to a long sample line.
In Horizontal: confirm pill stays inside the frame outline.
Toggle to Vertical: confirm pill still stays inside the frame outline (text wraps if needed).
Cold-launch the app (close it first).
Open Settings -> Captions; confirm the orientation select is set to Horizontal.

AC-003-a.notes: Retargeted after caption-profiles-persistence Slice B landed the real caption_layout tests. Slice A's orientation-designer UI is backed by the desktop/mobile default-profile contract; this test asserts the two defaults are distinct (AC-001-a in Slice B PRD). See journal.md for retarget history.

steps: Run git diff --stat src-tauri/src/managers/captions/ on the feature branch.
Confirm zero lines changed (this PR must not touch the backend caption manager).
Run git diff --stat src-tauri/src/commands/ | rg caption; confirm no new caption Tauri command was added.
Read the React diff for CaptionSettings.tsx; confirm orientation lives only in useState, never passed to invoke() / a Tauri command.
From repo root run: rg "caption-preview-frame" src
Confirm zero matches.
Confirm src/assets/caption-preview-frame.png does not exist on disk.
